behaviors.description: A static prompt-template lint check rejects phase-specific instruction leakage copied from SKILL.md.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: The failure is local to lint logic (detecting forbidden phase-instruction text in a template), not a cross-module handoff failure. REAL-INFRA: No OS/network/subprocess is required. MOCK-HIDE: Mocks are not the issue; the key risk is bad matching logic, which unit inputs expose directly. LLM-DEP: No model output dependency. MULTI-STEP: No multi-agentic sequence. REJECTION-GATE: Integration was considered, but behavior text says "static prompt-template lint check," which disqualifies needing full harness wiring as minimum.
behaviors.test_description: Call the lint function with a clean template and with a template containing copied phase instructions from SKILL.md; assert the leaked template is rejected with a precise violation reason and location.

behaviors.description: The fully assembled prompt produced by the real test harness (after interpolation and fixtures) still contains no phase-instruction leakage.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: Leakage can be introduced at module boundaries (template interpolation + fixture merge + final prompt assembly), so single-function tests can miss it. LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF: A unit test of just template rendering would miss leakage injected by fixture interpolation in later assembly stages. REAL-INFRA: No real network/subprocess required. LLM-DEP: No model judgment dependency. MULTI-STEP: No multi-agentic chain. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no real OS/network/subprocess requirement, (b) no AI/ML output quality dependency, (c) no multi-agentic chain, (d) no LLM-stage orchestration correctness under test.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real prompt assembly path with production-like fixtures and interpolation, capture the final prompt string, and assert it contains none of the forbidden phase-instruction snippets from SKILL.md.

behaviors.description: The test invocation loads skill context from plugin/skill files and validates behavior from that context rather than from hard-coded prompt rules.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: This is specifically a wiring/provenance failure across loader + invocation + validation modules. LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF: Unit-testing validator logic alone would miss a real bug where invocation silently uses hard-coded defaults instead of loaded skill files. REAL-INFRA: Local filesystem access is sufficient; no OS-level primitives beyond normal file I/O. LLM-DEP: Behavior here is about context source correctness, not model-output quality. MULTI-STEP: No required multi-agentic sequence. REJECTION-GATE: Agentic was considered, but behavior text focuses on loading/validation source-of-truth, not correctness of model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the real invocation path with a skill file containing a unique sentinel instruction and assert the harness-derived context reflects that sentinel (and not hard-coded fallback rules) in validation inputs.

behaviors.description: The harness can run the same user-style prompt against an intentionally degraded or missing SKILL.md variant without changing test prompt text.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: This checks run-configuration handoff (prompt constant, skill variant changes) across harness modules, not isolated logic. LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF: A unit test of prompt builder cannot catch a runner bug that mutates prompt text when switching skill variants. REAL-INFRA: No real network/subprocess required. LLM-DEP: Minimum behavior is execution setup invariance, not model quality. MULTI-STEP: No multi-agentic chain required for this behavior itself. REJECTION-GATE: Agentic was considered, but behavior text is about harness ability to run variants with unchanged prompt text, which can be validated without relying on model judgment.
behaviors.test_description: Run two harness executions (baseline vs degraded/missing SKILL.md variant) with identical user-style prompt fixture; assert prompt text/hash is unchanged across runs while skill source differs and both invocations are created correctly.

behaviors.description: When the relevant SKILL.md section is removed or degraded, the behavioral test fails under the same user-style prompt.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: The intended failure is that model behavior degrades when skill context degrades; this is invisible with deterministic stubs. LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF: Integration with mocked LLM can still pass because canned outputs ignore removed SKILL.md guidance, masking the regression. REAL-INFRA: No special OS/subprocess beyond normal call path, but real model execution is required. LLM-DEP: Correctness directly depends on what the real LLM produces under changed skill context. MULTI-STEP: Typically a single model decision path, so Workflow is not minimum. REJECTION-GATE: Workflow was considered, but behavior text describes one behavioral test outcome under one prompt, not multiple agentic stages in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run the same behavioral prompt against baseline skill and degraded/removed-skill variants using live model calls; assert baseline passes structural checks and degraded variant fails at least once under repeated trials with a predefined pass-rate threshold.

behaviors.description: Raw model output is captured to a diagnostic artifact before any JSON parsing or extraction logic runs.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: Ordering failure occurs at module interaction boundary (model response handler -> artifact writer -> parser). LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF: Unit-testing parser or writer in isolation misses orchestrator bugs where parsing runs first and raw text is lost on parse failure. REAL-INFRA: Local filesystem artifact writing is sufficient; no real network/subprocess needed to detect ordering bug. LLM-DEP: Content quality from LLM is irrelevant; only pipeline ordering matters. MULTI-STEP: Not a multi-agentic decision chain. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no real OS/network/subprocess dependency beyond standard local file I/O, (b) no AI/ML output-quality dependency, (c) no chained agentic steps, (d) no LLM-stage decision handoff correctness under test.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke response-processing pipeline with a representative raw model text and an extraction step that can fail; assert diagnostic artifact is written with unmodified raw output before parser/extractor execution and still exists when parse fails.

behaviors.description: Assertions enforce structural behavior constraints (for example schema paths and role-specific field placement), not only keyword presence checks.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: This behavior targets assertion logic itself (schema/path/placement checks), a local function/object concern. REAL-INFRA: No OS/network/subprocess needed. MOCK-HIDE: External mocks are not central; crafted payload fixtures are enough to expose failures. LLM-DEP: No dependence on live model judgment for this behavior definition. MULTI-STEP: No multi-agentic chain. REJECTION-GATE: Integration was considered, but behavior text is about assertion semantics, which can be validated directly with synthetic inputs.
behaviors.test_description: Unit-test assertion helpers with payloads that contain correct keywords but incorrect schema paths/field placements; assert structural validator fails those cases and passes correctly structured payloads.

behaviors.description: Under live model execution, structural assertions catch regressions that a keyword-only check would miss.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: Real regressions arise from model output variation where keywords appear but structure is wrong; canned mocks can hide this. LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF: Integration with fixed fixtures would not reveal model-produced malformed structure that still satisfies keyword checks. REAL-INFRA: Core requirement is live model execution. LLM-DEP: Correctness depends on actual model outputs and their variability. MULTI-STEP: Can be done in a single generation/assertion step, so Workflow is not minimum. REJECTION-GATE: Workflow was considered, but behavior text does not require multiple sequential model decisions, only one live execution compared across assertion styles.
behaviors.test_description: Run live model behavioral test with structural and keyword-only evaluators over the same responses; assert at least one case where keyword-only passes but structural assertions fail, demonstrating regression detection unique to structural checks.
